summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Plate Boundaries (Convergent, Divergent, Transform)
summary_sections.content_html:
Earth's lithosphere is divided into tectonic plates that interact at three types of plate boundaries:

  • Divergent: plates move apart. Magma rises to fill the gap, creating new crust. Examples: Mid-Atlantic Ridge, East African Rift.
  • Convergent: plates collide.
    • Oceanic–oceanic: denser plate subducts → deep-ocean trenches, volcanic island arcs
    • Oceanic–continental: oceanic plate subducts → mountain ranges, volcanoes (Andes)
    • Continental–continental: neither subducts → folded mountain ranges (Himalayas)
  • Transform: plates slide horizontally past each other. Produces earthquakes but no volcanism. Example: San Andreas Fault.
